# Service Terms — Reference Excerpt

*Effective date: 2026-01-01. This is a hand-built reference excerpt for testing; not a real legal agreement.*

## 1. Definitions

In these Terms, "Service" means the hosted analytics platform
provided by the Operator. "Customer" means the entity that has
accepted these Terms. "Authorized User" means an individual whom the
Customer has permitted to use the Service on the Customer's behalf.
"Content" means any data that the Customer or an Authorized User
submits to the Service.

## 2. License Grant

Subject to the Customer's compliance with these Terms, the Operator
grants the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable
license to access and use the Service during the Subscription Term.
The Customer may not sublicense the Service to any third party
without the Operator's prior written consent.

## 3. Customer Obligations

The Customer must not, and must not permit any Authorized User to:

- reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to derive the source code
  of the Service;
- use the Service to transmit unlawful, defamatory, or infringing
  Content;
- circumvent any technical limitation, rate limit, or access control
  imposed by the Service.

The Customer is responsible for the acts and omissions of every
Authorized User as if they were the acts and omissions of the
Customer itself.

## 4. Data Protection

The Operator shall process Content only to provide and maintain the
Service. The Operator shall implement and maintain reasonable
technical and organizational measures to protect Content against
unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. The
Operator shall notify the Customer of any confirmed Security
Incident affecting Customer Content within seventy-two (72) hours of
discovery.

## 5. Term and Termination

These Terms remain in effect until terminated. The Customer may
terminate by providing thirty (30) days' written notice. The Operator
may suspend or terminate the Service immediately on the Customer's
material breach. On termination, the Customer's right to access the
Service ceases and the Operator shall, within ninety (90) days,
delete or return all Customer Content.

## 6. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, neither party shall be liable
to the other for any indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or
punitive damages arising out of these Terms. Each party's total
aggregate liability shall not exceed the fees paid by the Customer to
the Operator in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving
rise to the claim.

## 7. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction stated in
the Order Form, excluding its conflict-of-laws rules.
